introSection: Big Beast was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Edgewater Park in Detroit, Michigan, USA. The ride was reportedly designed by John A. Miller, although at least one source credits Harry A. Browne.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened as Giant Thriller in 1927. At times, the ride was also referred to as Soul Train and Swinger. At least one source also claims it operated under the names Yellow Streak, The Rock, Mind Blower, Chicken Hills, and Wilde Beast.

It was relocated within the park in 1929.

The ride was damaged by a fire in 1955 and rebuilt by National Amusement Devices for the 1956 season. Modifications were made to the ride at this time, including new trains.

The ride and the rest of the park were leveled by spring 1982. Parts of the ride were reportedly sold to Paradise Lake in Salesville, Ohio, USA and were intended for a large new roller coaster.

sections.title: Incidents
sections.content:
A 17-year-old fell from the ride and was crushed on April 25, 1937.

A 25-year-old was fatally injured after falling from the ride on June 11, 1950.

fullText:
Big Beast was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Edgewater Park in Detroit, Michigan, USA. The ride was reportedly designed by John A. Miller, although at least one source credits Harry A. Browne.

The ride opened as Giant Thriller in 1927. At times, the ride was also referred to as Soul Train and Swinger. At least one source also claims it operated under the names Yellow Streak, The Rock, Mind Blower, Chicken Hills, and Wilde Beast.

It was relocated within the park in 1929.

The ride was damaged by a fire in 1955 and rebuilt by National Amusement Devices for the 1956 season. Modifications were made to the ride at this time, including new trains.

The ride and the rest of the park were leveled by spring 1982. Parts of the ride were reportedly sold to Paradise Lake in Salesville, Ohio, USA and were intended for a large new roller coaster.

A 17-year-old fell from the ride and was crushed on April 25, 1937.

A 25-year-old was fatally injured after falling from the ride on June 11, 1950.
